About Roel

Engineering is technical work. Great engineering is human work.

I am a software engineer at Team Rockstars IT, coach, speaker, proud father, and Zen teacher. My work explores how strong technology and strong humans can grow together.

Current work

Software Engineer at Team Rockstars IT

I build software, explore thoughtful AI integration, and help make the decisions, team dynamics, and engineering practices around the code more explicit.

Connect on LinkedIn

I thrive on both creating and connecting. Writing software gives me the joy of solving complex problems; coaching, teaching, and speaking let me work with the human dynamics behind those problems.

At Team Rockstars IT, I work as a software engineer while developing a broader path around mindful engineering, AI integration, and personal leadership. I am interested in what happens when high-performance code meets high-performance humans, especially when speed and automation begin to challenge ownership, focus, and craft.

What guides my work

I want technology to make people and teams more capable, not more disconnected. Sometimes that means writing better software. Sometimes it means making architecture decisions explicit, creating shared understanding, or slowing down long enough to see the problem behind the problem.

For me, good engineering creates clarity and trust. AI can accelerate the work, but it cannot replace judgment, discipline, or care; often, it reveals where those things were missing. Leadership is part of the same practice: creating the conditions in which people can decide, grow, and build together with confidence.

Technology with a human centre

I am enthusiastic about AI and the opportunities it creates. I am equally interested in what we should not outsource: judgment, curiosity, connection, and the struggle through which mastery develops. My aim is not to slow innovation down, but to help people stay awake and intentional inside it.

Learning in public

Zen, coaching, engineering, and fatherhood all keep teaching me versions of the same lesson: growth rarely comes from having everything under control. It comes from paying attention, speaking honestly, and being willing to step forward before you feel completely ready.

This website is where I share that ongoing practice. Some articles are technical, some deeply personal, but all of them ask how we can create meaningful work without losing ourselves in the process.

At work Software engineering, AI integration, and team dynamics
In practice Coaching, speaking, personal leadership, and Zen
Off-screen Fatherhood, board games, cycling, and Lego
The digital zen garden

Build with clarity. Lead with care.

Software engineer, coach, speaker, and Zen teacher exploring how technology, leadership, and attention can help people build with more clarity, trust, and care.
